compiled_state.title: [inbound-bridge]: fetch returns empty since 2026-04-16 refactor -> 7+ days of zero syncs with success exit codes
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Expected Behavior

The Inbound Bridge workflow pulls Jira issues every 30 minutes. For each new Jira issue matching the JQL, it writes either a CREATE event or a BRIDGE_ALERT event to the tickets directory. The scheduled run commits new events and advances the checkpoint only after a successful round trip.

#### Actual Behavior

Since 2026-04-16 16:13 UTC, every scheduled run reports status=success and advances the inbound-checkpoint, but zero ticket event files are produced. The last ticket files written (BRIDGE_ALERT events for DIG-1555..1559, each with reasons "Unknown type value: 'Bug'" and "Unknown status value: 'To Do'") occurred at that timestamp. Over 200 bridge commits since then only touch the checkpoint file.

This was discovered when bug DIG-1564 (existing in Jira) never appeared locally. Review of 100 recent run logs confirmed no mention of DIG-1564 — the fetch is returning empty.

### Root Cause Hypothesis

A cluster of refactor commits on 2026-04-16 extracted bridge-inbound.py logic into the bridge/ subpackage:

- 14:40 PDT | 9160ed57 | Extract Python files >1000 lines into focused subpackages
- 15:23 PDT | 3e903754 | Extract 7 handlers from bridge-outbound
- 15:33 PDT | 05e3464a | Extract process_inbound handlers
- 15:36 PDT | f168ec73 | Merge of same
- 16:16 PDT | a767e6ff | Restore bridge-inbound.py to correct post-extraction state (-675 lines)

The last successful alert write happened at 09:13 PDT, before the refactor. The exact failure mode inside _fetch_issues_with_checkpoint > fetch_jira_changes > acli_client.search_issues is unknown because bridge-inbound.py emits no stdout/stderr on success. Possibilities include: broken symbol resolution from the bridge package after extraction, silent failure path in ACLI returning an empty list without raising, or JQL construction defect.

### Hidden Failure Mode

The script exits 0 in all cases where search_issues returns an empty list. _write_success_checkpoint advances the cursor unconditionally on any non-exception completion. The GHA workflow then marks the run success. Because the checkpoint advances regardless of whether any issues were actually fetched, the failure is silent and irreversible without manual checkpoint rewind.

### Impact

- DIG-1560+ tickets never synced to local
- All Jira status/type/edit changes in that window lost (no STATUS/EDIT events written either)
- Monitoring was ineffective because GHA reports success for all runs

### Required Fixes

1. Add observability: log fetched-issue count, JQL window, BRIDGE_ENV_ID, and per-handler summary.
2. Add a bright-line assertion: if the window is >30 minutes wide and returns zero issues, write a diagnostic WARN to stderr.
3. Identify and fix the actual regression (diff bridge-inbound.py and bridge/_inbound_api.py pre/post a767e6ff for function signature drift or import ordering issues).
4. Rewind the checkpoint to 2026-04-16T16:13:00Z after the fix lands so DIG-1560+ are re-fetched.

comments.body:
CHECKPOINT REWIND — manual step required

The session-internal attempt to rewind the checkpoint was blocked by the tickets-tracker-guard hook (by design: direct edits to the tickets directory are prevented). The rewind needs to happen via a new CLI subcommand OR an out-of-band manual edit.

Manual rewind (run from a terminal without the hook active):

```
cd /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.tickets-tracker
git pull origin tickets
printf '%s\n' '{"last_pull_ts": "2026-04-16T16:13:00Z", "last_run_id": "rewind-after-bug-0e38-a5da"}' > .inbound-checkpoint.json
git add .inbound-checkpoint.json
git commit -m "chore: rewind inbound checkpoint to 2026-04-16T16:13:00Z (bug 0e38-a5da)"
git push origin tickets
```

After the fix for bug 0e38-a5da lands and the checkpoint is rewound, the next scheduled run will re-fetch DIG-1560+ (and any other issues updated between 2026-04-16T16:13:00Z and now).

Follow-up: add a "ticket rewind-inbound-checkpoint <iso-timestamp>" subcommand so future rewinds don't require editing the event-sourced state by hand.
comments.author: Worktree

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 6 (ADVANCED). Cluster mode with 44a0-3a13. Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed.
comments.author: Worktree
